ogTitle: Glass House Mountains vs Glastonbury: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Glass House Mountains and Glastonbury.
heading: Glass House Mountains vs Glastonbury — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Glass House Mountains, Queensland and Glastonbury, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Glastonbury has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Glass House Mountains (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Glass House Mountains or Glastonbury safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Glass House Mountains has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Glastonbury scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Glass House Mountains scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Glastonbury scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Glass House Mountains or Glastonbury?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Glastonbury has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
